I read the prompt and prior log as an external proposal record. The prompt fixes the special tame-format submission, and the prior log leaves the dynamic tame-inertia word as the only serious unrefuted candidate after earlier scalar/plain-ŌÄ2/fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 variants were killed by finite-quotient tests.

The live candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ‚ąíő∑ is irrelevant here because that exponent is only applied to ŌĄ, whose finite tame images have odd order.

Stable algebraic conventions I agree with Turns 53‚Äď54 that the recurring reciprocal ambiguity should be eliminated by writing equations, not informal fractions.For the sign double cover V=kerőļ, let t be the deck involution and work on a nontrivial odd tame-character block ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1. ForNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ,the Fox coefficient isDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j.

It satisfies (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąít,so the usable identity isDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.

Thus Da is radical, because 1‚ąít is radical and 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit. Therefore the coefficient of the correction factor x‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. This keeps the nontrivial tame-character Smith blocks unit-pivot blocks.For the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

The elimination polynomial Ps (T) is defined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.This is the safe convention; writing the reciprocal is not legitimate in F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1). With this convention the earlier cup-block nonsingularity proof survives.New computation: the (e,f)=(5,4) first IA residual vanishes after the correct affine normalization I pushed the exact next target from Turn 54: compute the candidate‚Äôs residual first marked IA class in the tame quotientőĒ5,4=‚ü®ŌĄ,ŌÉ‚ą£ŌĄ5=1,¬†ŌÉ4=1,¬†ŌÉŌĄŌÉ‚ąí1=ŌĄ2‚ü©.For degree 2, one may use the finite approximation ŌÄ=5, since 5‚Č°0(mod5) and 5‚Č°1(mod4), and ‚ąíő∑‚Č°‚ąí2‚Č°3(mod5). The candidate word becomesr=xŌÉ‚ąí1xŌÉx‚ąí1(xŌĄ3)5 ŌÉ(yŌĄ)5ŌÉ‚ąí1y‚ąí1.

Using Reidemeister‚ÄďSchreier generatorsXi,a=XŌĄiŌÉa,Yi,a=YŌĄiŌÉa,S=ŌÉ4,with i‚ąąZ/5 and a‚ąąZ/4, the linear relations have rank 19, leaving 20+2 generators as expected. A convenient invariant degree-one class isb=Y0,0X0,3 in multiplicative notation, i.e. b=Y0,0+X0,3 in L1. This class is fixed by both ŌĄ and ŌÉ in L1.

The important catch is that the raw group lift Y0,0X0,3 is only linearly H-fixed, where H=‚ü®ŌĄ‚ü©. Its raw degree-two ŌĄ-error is nonzero. Because ‚ą£H‚ą£=5 is odd, this is harmless but must be corrected: there is a unique augmentation correction c‚ąąL2, modulo H-invariants, satisfying (ŌĄ‚ąí1)c=ŌĄ(b)b‚ąí1 in¬†L2.

Replacing b by the corrected lift bc, the ŌÉ-IA term becomesmbcorr=mb+(ŌÉ‚ąí1)c.This is the first new bookkeeping point: without this H-equivariant correction, the raw mb‚ąíőėb is not even H-invariant and therefore cannot represent a class in H1(P,L2H).There is a second normalization issue. In these naive coordinates, the actual quadratic DemuŇ°kin relation is not simplyg‚ąĎXg[2]+[b,S].The degree-two relation computed from the product of the 20 conjugate relators isqact=g‚ąąőĒ5,4‚ąĎXg[2]+g‚ąąőĒ5,4‚ąĎ[Xg,XgŌÉ]+[b,S].Thus the canonical affine particular solution for the b-component norm equation is not Turn 54‚Äôs square-only h‚ąąH‚ąĎXh[2].

It must include the H-coset part of the adjacent-edge term:őėbact=h‚ąąH‚ąĎXh[2]+h‚ąąH‚ąĎ[Xh,XhŌÉ].

Indeed,NP(őėbact)=g‚ąąőĒ‚ąĎXg[2]+g‚ąąőĒ‚ąĎ[Xg,XgŌÉ]=[S,b]using qact=0.With both corrections made, the residual homogeneous first IA class for the candidate ismbcorr‚ąíőėbact.

My computation givesmbcorr‚ąíőėbact=0 already in the chosen degree-two coordinates, not merely modulo the expected seven-dimensional ambiguity space.So in the (5,4) quotient, after the correct H-equivariant lift and the correct affine origin from the actual quadratic relation, the candidate selects the zero homogeneous residual class inH1(P,L2H),P=őĒ5,4/H‚ČÉC4.This is stronger than merely saying ‚Äúno contradiction was found‚ÄĚ: the seven first-IA directions from prior turns are real as a difference space, but the candidate lies at the canonical affine origin in this test.

Consequence This changes the next arithmetic comparison. Earlier turns framed the (5,4) target as ‚Äúcompute seven candidate coordinates, then compare them with arithmetic.‚ÄĚ The corrected formulation is sharper:choose the same H-equivariant lift of the invariant b-class;use the actual quadratic relation qact, not the already-normalized DemuŇ°kin form;subtract őėbact;compare the arithmetic residual with zero in H1(P,L2H).In other words, a future arithmetic calculation should not compare raw mb coordinates. It should compare the normalized residual. For the candidate, that residual is zero in the first substantial mixed test (e,f)=(5,4).

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. In the (e,f)=(5,4) quotient, I computed the candidate‚Äôs first marked IA residual after two necessary normalizations: an H-equivariant correction of the b-lift, and replacement of the square-only affine origin byőėbact=h‚ąąH‚ąĎXh[2]+h‚ąąH‚ąĎ[Xh,XhŌÉ].

With these corrections,mbcorr‚ąíőėbact=0.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient remains a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, showing that the candidate‚Äôs full filtered tame action agrees with arithmetic over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: Repeat the normalized residual computation for the next mixed cases, especially (e,f)=(17,8) or (91,12), where the first IA ambiguity has many more involution-indexed directions. The target is whether the candidate again lands at the canonical affine origin after using the actual quadratic relation.[BREAKTHROUGH]
